Witness: Represented that LJM bore real economic risk on its Enron deals.
Sold ~$15M of Enron stock in Sept 2001 because of post-9/11 market fear.
On resigning (Aug 2001) Enron was in the strongest shape it had ever been in.
LJM/Chewco management was presented as legitimate fund administration.
Certified Enron's financial statements as GAAP-compliant.
Characterized Enron's mark-to-market accounting as conservative.
LJM partnerships were arm's-length and managed to avoid any conflict.
Claimed limited knowledge of the Raptor hedging vehicles' problems.
